parent.policy_statement: Implement Overarching Strategies for the Austin Climate Equity Plan (green jobs; prioritize local initiatives; regional collaboration; carbon reduction/removal/offsets).
parent.verbatim_text:
OVERARCHING STRATEGIES

## Green Jobs and Entrepreneurship

- Create green jobs and entrepreneurship opportunities...

## Prioritize Local Community Initiatives

- Recognize and support existing community-led organizations...

## Regional Collaboration

- Create a Texas Climate Collaborative...

## Local Carbon Reduction Projects, Carbon Dioxide Removal, and Carbon Offsets

- Establish clear support and prioritization for local greenhouse gas reduction and carbon removal projects...
parent.extraction_rationale: A named umbrella set of cross-cutting strategies presented as a grouped program with multiple sub-strategies listed beneath.

subs.policy_statement: Create green jobs and entrepreneurship opportunities that advance the plan's goals and expand economic opportunity and inclusion in low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Green Jobs and Entrepreneurship

- Create green jobs and entrepreneurship opportunities that advance the goals of this plan, expand economic opportunity and inclusion, and build agency and decision-making power in low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.parent_policy_name: Implement Overarching Strategies for the Austin Climate Equity Plan (green jobs; prioritize local initiatives; regional collaboration; carbon reduction/removal/offsets).
subs.section_header: OVERARCHING STRATEGIES
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to create jobs/entrepreneurship opportunities, framed as an overarching strategy.

subs.policy_statement: Recognize and support existing community-led organizations, businesses, and programs to help achieve plan goals while building a green and just economy and culture.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Prioritize Local Community Initiatives

- Recognize and support existing community-led organizations, businesses, and programs that can help achieve the goals in this plan while building a green and just economy and culture.
subs.parent_policy_name: Implement Overarching Strategies for the Austin Climate Equity Plan (green jobs; prioritize local initiatives; regional collaboration; carbon reduction/removal/offsets).
subs.section_header: OVERARCHING STRATEGIES
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention to support community-led initiatives, stated as an overarching strategy.

subs.policy_statement: Create a Texas Climate Collaborative linking elected officials, City staff, and utility staff and work with nearby cities and five-county governments on climate plan implementation.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Regional Collaboration

- Create a Texas Climate Collaborative linking elected officials, City staff, and utility staff working to implement recently adopted climate plans in San Antonio, Houston, Dallas, and Austin. Work with cities across the state and neighboring cities... and the five-county governments: Travis, Williamson, Hays, Bastrop, and Caldwell.
subs.parent_policy_name: Implement Overarching Strategies for the Austin Climate Equity Plan (green jobs; prioritize local initiatives; regional collaboration; carbon reduction/removal/offsets).
subs.section_header: OVERARCHING STRATEGIES
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete institutional collaboration commitment (create collaborative; named participants/region).

subs.policy_statement: Establish clear support and prioritization for local greenhouse gas reduction and carbon removal projects, carbon dioxide removal, and carbon offsets when necessary.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Local Carbon Reduction Projects, Carbon Dioxide Removal, and Carbon Offsets

- Establish clear support and prioritization for local greenhouse gas reduction and carbon removal projects, carbon dioxide removal, and carbon offsets when necessary.
subs.parent_policy_name: Implement Overarching Strategies for the Austin Climate Equity Plan (green jobs; prioritize local initiatives; regional collaboration; carbon reduction/removal/offsets).
subs.section_header: OVERARCHING STRATEGIES
subs.extraction_rationale: Defines a strategic approach to carbon reductions/removal/offsets as a plan commitment.

section_header: new 2030 climate equity goals for austin — sustainable buildings

parent.policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
parent.verbatim_text:
## Sustainable Buildings

- Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
parent.section_header: NEW 2030 CLIMATE EQUITY GOALS FOR AUSTIN — Sustainable Buildings
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified 2030 building-sector targets; followed by multiple numbered strategies in the Sustainable Buildings section.

subs.policy_statement: Ensure benefits of building decarbonization programs flow to low-income communities and communities of color through comprehensive energy poverty mitigation and partnerships.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Ensure benefits flow to low-income communities and communities of color

Pursue a comprehensive energy poverty mitigation strategy by partnering with trusted community organizations, affordable housing developers, and schools in equitable outreach and program development.
subs.parent_policy_name: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (energy poverty mitigation strategy and partnerships) tied to the buildings goal.

subs.policy_statement: Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy consumption via outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanded benchmarking for existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Enhance understanding of energy consumption

Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy savings opportunities, benefits, and climate impacts of energy consumption. This will be done through direct outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanding benchmarking requirements for all existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
subs.parent_policy_name: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete programmatic actions (benchmarking expansion; data access; outreach).

subs.policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon building milestones through new building energy codes/amendments and incentives/education for existing building decarbonization, in collaboration with utilities and with equity focus.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Achieve energy-efficient, net-zero carbon buildings

Achieve goal milestones for net-zero carbon buildings through new building energy codes, amendments, and other methods. Engage owners and operators of existing buildings to decarbonize through incentives and education for contractors and occupants. Collaborate with local utilities to implement equitable emission reduction strategies.
subs.parent_policy_name: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific policy mechanisms (codes, amendments, incentives, education, utility collaboration).

subs.policy_statement: Create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging building-related technologies by partnering with local unions and education/advocacy organizations serving low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Ensure equitable workforce development for emerging technologies

Prioritize investment in local emissions reduction and create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging technologies by partnering with local unions, education, and advocacy organizations that serve low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.parent_policy_name: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
subs.extraction_rationale: Defines a workforce development intervention with specified partners and equity focus.

parent.policy_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
parent.verbatim_text: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions-reduction target with a deadline; followed by a list of strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting and high-GWP refrigerants to increase capture and safe destruction.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Capture and destroy old refrigerants

Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting substances and global warming potential (GWP) refrigerants.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific program intervention (destruction program with pricing mechanism).

subs.policy_statement: Move forward with building code amendments and other local actions to allow/encourage low and no-GWP refrigerants as soon as feasible.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Improve building codes to encourage cleaner refrigerants

Closely follow developments in revised building codes that allow the use of low and no GWP refrigerants... and move forward with code amendments and other local action as soon as feasible.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
subs.extraction_rationale: Regulatory/code change commitment related to refrigerants.

subs.policy_statement: Create incentives for refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair in partnership with food retail, restaurants, warehouses, and HVAC companies, prioritizing outreach to small businesses owned by people of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Create incentives for leak detection and repair

Partner with grocery stores, convenience stores, restaurants... to create an incentive for designing and tracking refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete incentive-based policy with named partner sectors.

subs.policy_statement: Create an awareness, education, and training campaign for HVAC service providers and related stakeholders; lead by example with refrigerant leak detection/repair at City-owned facilities.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Awareness and training for HVAC service providers

Create an awareness, education, and training campaign... Lead by example by implementing a refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair program at City-owned facilities.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific campaign plus a City operational commitment (lead by example).

subs.policy_statement: Reduce the volume/use of refrigerants, particularly high-GWP refrigerants, by expanding and prioritizing codes and incentives favoring passive design, reduction, and efficiency.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Reduce the volume of refrigerants

Emphasize the link between design and refrigerant use by reducing and preventing the use of refrigerants... particularly those with high GWP.

How we'll get there:

- Ensure codes and incentives that favor passive design, reduction, and efficiency are expanded and prioritized.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete policy direction to use codes/incentives to reduce refrigerant use.

parent.policy_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
parent.verbatim_text:
By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline*.

*The embodied carbon associated with the construction and materials must not exceed 500 kg CO2e/m2 (~100 lbs. CO2e/sf) per project.
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified target plus specific performance threshold; followed by multiple strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to deliver healthy, low-carbon buildings, prioritizing community facilities serving low-income communities and communities of color first.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Lead by example through design and construction standards

In partnership with other cities and states, develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to result in healthy, low-carbon buildings.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific government procurement/standards action (lead by example).

subs.policy_statement: Integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs (e.g., expedited permitting and Austin Energy Green Building) and transition voluntary guidance into planning/development agreements over time.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Incentivize lower-carbon materials

Enhance and integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs, like the expedited permitting process and Austin Energy's Green Building program, to transition voluntary design guidance into planning and development agreements over time.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear incentive/program modification commitment.

subs.policy_statement: Create a performance framework and educational programming on low-carbon materials best practices for industry professionals and the public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Educate stakeholders on materials best practices

Create a performance framework and educational programming for industry professionals and the general public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color, to reduce the lifecycle and negative health impacts of building materials and construction practices.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific education/programming intervention tied to embodied carbon and health impacts.

subs.policy_statement: Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact building materials (e.g., glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, carpet).
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Stimulate decarbonization with local producers installation

Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact materials, specifically glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, and carpet.
subs.parent_policy_name: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention focusing on decarbonizing named material categories via partnerships.

parent.policy_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
parent.verbatim_text: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified water-demand target with timeframe and implementation basis; followed by strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Enhance community engagement and partnerships to advance Water Forward equity strategies (e.g., My ATX Water, incentives, ordinances) and implement targeted outreach to improve participation.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Engage residents in water efficiency technological transitions, and conservation programs

Enhance community engagement strategies and create partnerships with community organizations to advance equity through Water Forward strategies, including My ATX Water (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), incentives, and ordinances.
subs.parent_policy_name: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific engagement and partnership actions to implement water conservation strategies with equity focus.

subs.policy_statement: Evaluate water conservation and customer assistance program criteria to remove structural barriers and expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and other low-income-serving programs.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Evaluate water conservation, customer assistance, and workforce development program participation criteria

Evaluate program criteria to identify opportunities to address structural barriers that prevent program participation... explore strategies to expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and increase participation in other programs serving low-income customers.
subs.parent_policy_name: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete program evaluation and expansion commitment for equity in water assistance and conservation.

subs.policy_statement: Assess and pursue water conservation and energy management optimization at the water-energy nexus, including evaluating Austin Water facility energy use and developing methods to quantify emissions reductions from water-demand reduction.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Reduce emissions at the water-energy nexus

Assess how water demand reduction is associated with energy consumption in residential and commercial buildings and Austin Water facilities. Identify and pursue synergistic water conservation and energy management optimization efforts through programs and partnerships.
subs.parent_policy_name: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
subs.section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific assessment and optimization actions linking water conservation to emissions reductions.

parent.policy_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
parent.verbatim_text: By 2030, 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified land-use development siting target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Adopt Complete Communities plans to reverse historical and racist impacts, address displacement, and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Plan for complete communities

Adopt Complete Communities plans that reverse historical and racist impacts on areas experiencing displacement and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
subs.parent_policy_name: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific planning instrument commitment (adopt plans) tied to equity and land use.

subs.policy_statement: Work with medium and large employers to locate places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Work with employers on location and amenities

Work with medium and large employers to locate their places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
subs.parent_policy_name: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit intervention to influence employer location decisions toward target areas.

subs.policy_statement: Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops with first/last-mile options.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3 : Create mobility hubs

Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops to offer a complete trip experience. These hubs should offer various first- and last-mile mobility options.
subs.parent_policy_name: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/program concept to improve multimodal access.

subs.policy_statement: Phase out free employee parking at City facilities and other large employers within 1/2 mile of the transit priority network, replacing it with cash-out, carpool/vanpool, transit passes, telework, or other commuter benefits.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Phase out free parking

Phase out the practice of providing free parking spaces to employees at City of Austin facilities and other large employers located within 1/2 mile of the Austin Strategic Mobility Plan's transit priority network. Instead, offer a parking cash-out, carpool and vanpool options, flex schedules, transit passes, support for teleworking, or other commuter benefits programs.
subs.parent_policy_name: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific policy change with defined scope and alternative measures.

parent.policy_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
parent.verbatim_text: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable housing units*, with 75% of new housing located within ½ mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified housing production/preservation target with timeframe and location share; followed by strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Engage communities vulnerable to displacement for immediate affordable housing assistance and proactively monitor at-risk affordable properties to extend affordability.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Offer immediate affordable housing assistance

Engage directly with communities that are vulnerable to displacement and connect them with services. Proactively monitor affordable housing properties at risk of losing their affordability status to extend the period of affordability.
subs.parent_policy_name: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
subs.extraction_rationale: Direct programmatic commitment for anti-displacement and affordability preservation.

subs.policy_statement: Increase funding for the City's Housing Trust Fund and support capacity building for community development corporations.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Fund affordable housing

Increase funding for the City's current Housing Trust Fund and support capacity building for community development corporations.
subs.parent_policy_name: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
subs.extraction_rationale: Resource allocation commitment (increase funding) and institutional capacity building.

subs.policy_statement: Include impacted low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities in Housing Investment Review Committee activities and in creating anti-displacement programs, and enhance culturally relevant outreach for subsidized affordable units in gentrifying areas.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Enhance community engagement for affordable housing and antidisplacement programs

Include low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities directly affected by systemic inequalities in the City's Housing Investment Review Committee activities and when creating anti-displacement programs.
subs.parent_policy_name: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
subs.extraction_rationale: Governance/participation mechanism commitment for equitable housing program design and outreach.

parent.policy_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
parent.verbatim_text: By 2030, 50% of trips in Austin are made using public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided altogether by working from home*.
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified mode-shift target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Expand and improve public transportation

Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific implementation commitment to deliver transit improvements through Project Connect.

subs.policy_statement: Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options and expand free transit options to increase access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Promote free transportation options

Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options.

How we'll get there:

- Expand free transit options, including through Capital Metro, to provide increased transportation access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete access policy (resources + expanding free transit).

subs.policy_statement: Engage impacted communities to improve transit stops/stations and enforce ADA and accessibility guidelines around transit and public spaces.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Enhance transit stations and stops

Partner with Capital Metro, developers, and community organizers to engage low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities to improve transit stops, stations, and access to these facilities.

How we'll get there:

- Enforce the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Public Rights-of-Way Accessibility Guidelines regulations...
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific implementation and enforcement commitments for equitable, accessible transit facilities.

subs.policy_statement: Implement 2020 Proposition B bonds for transportation infrastructure (sidewalks, bikeways, trails, safety, safe routes to school, substandard streets) in low-income communities and communities of color through meaningful engagement.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Prioritize bicycle networks

Implement the 2020 Proposition B voter-approved general obligation bonds for transportation infrastructure... in low-income communities and communities of color through a meaningful community engagement process.
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure investment implementation tied to a named funding mechanism (Proposition B).

subs.policy_statement: Provide access to free or reduced-price bicycles and bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve safety and comfort for riders of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Enhance bicycle education and training

Provide access to free or reduced-priced bicycles and basic bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve the comfort and safety of people of color riding bicycles.
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific program/training interventions with equity and safety focus.

subs.policy_statement: Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement, and build priority segments while addressing ADA barriers and gaps.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 6: Improve sidewalks, urban trails, and crossings

Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan, emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement.

How we'll get there:

- Build all high- and very-high-priority sidewalk and trail segments and address ADA barriers and gaps...
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete planning update and build-out commitments with accessibility requirements.

subs.policy_statement: Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies, including a City telework policy encouraging at least 85% of eligible City employees to participate and supporting a goal of 15% residents working from home by 2039.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 7: Promote remote and flexible work policies

Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies to maintain and encourage further reductions in single-occupancy vehicle trips.

How we'll get there:

- ...all new City of Austin telework and remote work policies contribute towards the goal of achieving 15% Austin residents working from home by 2039. This should include a strategy to implement a City of Austin telework policy encouraging no less than 85% of eligible City of Austin employees to participate in telework.
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific policy approach with quantifiable participation targets/timeframe.

parent.policy_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
parent.verbatim_text: By 2030, 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin are electrified, and electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse. This translates to approximately 460,000 electric vehicles on the road.
parent.extraction_rationale: Quantified electrification target with timeframe and scale estimate; followed by strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment to inform an EV adoption growth plan, using community partners and hiring residents from the communities served.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Conduct an EV Community Needs Assessment

Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment... The assessment will inform an EV adoption growth plan...
subs.parent_policy_name: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).

subs.policy_statement: Create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing EVs, prioritizing low-income communities and communities of color and areas with limited transit access.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Create equitable incentives for buying and leasing EVs

Collaborate with community partners to create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing electric vehicles. This strategy would... prioritize low-income communities and communities of color...
subs.parent_policy_name: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).

subs.policy_statement: Partner with mobility authorities to reduce or eliminate toll rates for low-income communities and communities of color who drive EVs on toll roads from the Eastern Crescent to central Austin.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Reduce tolls for EVs in the Eastern Crescent

Partner with highway and regional mobility authorities to offer a reduced or eliminated toll rate for low-income communities and communities of color who drive EVs on toll roads from the Eastern Crescent to central Austin.
subs.parent_policy_name: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).

subs.policy_statement: Create an electric bike and car-sharing program centered on low-income communities and communities of color, with income thresholds and actions to address displacement.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Launch an e-bike and electric car-sharing program

Create an electric bike and car-sharing program centered on low-income communities and communities of color to support functional, low-cost, zero-emissions mobility.
subs.parent_policy_name: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).

subs.policy_statement: Encourage public sector fleets in the five-county MSA to commit to buying 100% electric vehicles when available, cost-competitive, and operationally suitable, leveraging purchasing collaboration and considering early retirement of older vehicles.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Electrify public sector fleet vehicles

Encourage vehicle fleets from public sector agencies... to commit to buying 100% electric vehicles when they are available, cost-competitive, and meet operational needs.
subs.parent_policy_name: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).

parent.policy_statement: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
parent.verbatim_text: By 2030, Austin has a compelling and equitably distributed mix of level 1, 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city... could mean more than 37,000 charging ports.
parent.extraction_rationale: Time-bound infrastructure scale target; followed by detailed strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Incentivize installation of EV charging infrastructure by multiple actors and prioritize systematically excluded areas, including installing chargers on publicly owned land to fill gaps.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Create a network with more low-cost, accessible charging stations

Continue to incentivize the installation of EV charging infrastructure by the City, businesses, auto manufacturers, and third-party charging companies...

- Prioritize areas that have been systematically excluded... 
- Fill in gaps by installing EV charging on publicly owned land in systematically excluded areas...
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Equitably distributed charging by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure incentive and siting commitments with equity prioritization.

subs.policy_statement: Incentivize internet-connected smart charging to support grid reliability, efficiency, reduced emissions, accessibility, and lower costs by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Incentivize internet-connected smart charging

By 2030, the City will have a network of intelligent charging... Incentivize internet-connected charging infrastructure with the ability to manage the start and end time and charge rate across 24 hours...
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Equitably distributed charging by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific technology/incentive commitment with deadline (by 2030).

subs.policy_statement: Adopt new energy and building codes to address future EV charging needs and lower barriers, including potentially requiring chargers at commercial and multifamily properties and EV-ready single-family homes.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Adopt new energy and building codes

Adopt new energy and building codes that address future EV charging needs...

How we'll get there:

- Consider... requiring EV chargers at commercial and multifamily properties, and requiring single-family homes to be EV charger-ready.
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide an equitably distributed mix of Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of VMT by 2030 (potentially 37,000+ charging ports).
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Equitably distributed charging by 2030
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory/code-based intervention for EV readiness and charger requirements.

subs.policy_statement: Expand culturally competent outreach to systematically excluded groups and stakeholders about EV charging incentives and installation.
subs.verbatim_text:

Expand outreach to community groups, professional organizations, unions, and property managers using culturally competent information about EV charging incentives and installation.

subs.extraction_rationale: Specific outreach/communications strategy to increase equitable uptake.

parent.policy_statement: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits and support industry growth.
parent.verbatim_text: The Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area is a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits while supporting the growth of this emerging industry.
parent.extraction_rationale: Named regional leadership goal accompanied by multiple strategies (regional coalition, pilots, just transition, business ecosystem).

subs.policy_statement: Create a regional coalition within the five-county MSA to support EV adoption with inclusive government, business, and community stakeholders.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Create a regional coalition to support EVs

The City will take the lead in creating a regional coalition to support EV adoption within the fivecounty MSA.
subs.parent_policy_name: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits and support industry growth.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Regional leadership
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete governance action (City leads creation of coalition).

subs.policy_statement: Continue to pilot and be an early adopter of emerging transportation electrification technologies and ensure low-income communities and communities of color access benefits first, including pursuing grants and scaling successful pilots.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Pilot and adopt new technology

Austin will continue to pilot and be an early adopter of emerging technologies for transportation electrification and ensure that low-income communities and communities of color can access the benefits first.
subs.parent_policy_name: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits and support industry growth.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Regional leadership
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to pilots/early adoption with equity-first benefit access.

subs.policy_statement: Lead a just transition to electrified regional transportation by collaborating with labor, grassroots organizations, and businesses and ensuring low-income communities and communities of color benefit.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Prioritize a just transition

Austin will be a leader in the just transition to an electrified regional transportation system... We will ensure that low-income communities and communities of color are positioned to benefit from the switch to electric transportation.
subs.parent_policy_name: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits and support industry growth.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Regional leadership
subs.extraction_rationale: Just transition workforce/equity commitment tied to transportation electrification.

subs.policy_statement: Support economic development for new and existing local companies in the EV supply chain to create a robust electric transportation cluster in Central Texas, prioritizing diverse ownership and high-paying jobs for low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Expand the EV-related business ecosystem

Create a robust electric transportation economic cluster in Central Texas by supporting economic development for new and existing local companies focused on the EV supply chain...

- Prioritize high-paying jobs for low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.parent_policy_name: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits and support industry growth.
subs.section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: Regional leadership
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific economic development strategy linked to EV ecosystem and equity outcomes.

parent.policy_statement: Ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary/health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste by 2030.

parent.extraction_rationale: Time-bound system goal with specific features; followed by strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Apply a purchasing framework and build a regional food system network to increase institutional and corporate procurement of pro-climate, pro-health food options.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Support institutional food purchasing

Apply a purchasing framework, support supply-chain infrastructure, and build a regional food system network to bolster institutional and corporate food procurement of proclimate, pro-health options.

Implement community-driven programs to incentivize and promote more affordable and culturally relevant pro-climate, pro-health choices in prepared and retail food options, focusing on minimizing displacement.

subs.policy_statement: Conduct a food waste root cause analysis and implement changes to increase food waste reduction practices by 50%, covering single-family, multifamily, and commercial properties.
subs.verbatim_text:

Conduct a food waste root cause analysis and implement changes informed by the analysis to increase food waste reduction practices by 50%. These practices should... include single-family, multi-family, and commercial properties.
subs.parent_policy_name: Ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary/health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste by 2030.

parent.policy_statement: Legally protect an additional 20,000 acres of carbon pools on natural lands and manage all new and existing natural areas (~70,000 acres total) for resilience by 2030.

Identify additional woodland, grassland, and wetland systems for protection, focusing on new conservation lands in the Travis County Eastern Planning Areas while prioritizing benefits for lowincome communities and communities of color.

Prepare natural lands for climate change and avoid catastrophic loss of carbon pools through active, intentional, and holistic management.

subs.policy_statement: Promote no net loss of tree canopy on private developments by increasing tree protections and landscape regulations and/or creating incentives, including extending protections to extraterritorial jurisdiction.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Promote tree protections and landscape regulations

Promote no net loss of tree canopy on private developments by increasing tree protections and landscape regulation or working with developers and homeowner's associations to create incentives.
subs.parent_policy_name: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, focusing on increasing canopy cover equitably.
subs.section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: 50% tree canopy by 2050
subs.extraction_rationale: Regulatory/incentive approach to private development tree canopy protections.

subs.policy_statement: Increase City funding for community tree-planting programs focused on low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Increase community tree planting

Increase City funding for community tree-planting programs focused on low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.parent_policy_name: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, focusing on increasing canopy cover equitably.
subs.section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: 50% tree canopy by 2050
subs.extraction_rationale: Direct resource allocation commitment (increase City funding) targeted to equity populations.

subs.policy_statement: Create a tree, water, and soil management/resilience guide for private property and provide City technical and financial assistance for tree planting/care for residents and small businesses in low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Promote tree health and resilience on private and non-City public lands

Create a tree, water and soil management, and resilience guide for various types of private property. Provide City technical and financial assistance for tree planting and care for residents and small businesses in low-income communities and communities of color...
subs.parent_policy_name: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, focusing on increasing canopy cover equitably.
subs.section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: 50% tree canopy by 2050
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific guidance plus assistance commitments targeting equity groups.

parent.policy_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
parent.verbatim_text: By 2030, include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
parent.extraction_rationale: Time-bound City land management requirement with emissions outcome (neutral/negative); followed by strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Prioritize carbon neutrality and community benefits in public land acquisition and management, including completing land management database and using carbon-negative/low-carbon practices.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Prioritize carbon neutrality for public lands

Prioritize carbon neutrality and community benefits in land acquisition and management practices for public lands.
subs.parent_policy_name: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
subs.section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific management and acquisition prioritization commitment.

subs.policy_statement: Reclaim underused public spaces to increase access and ecological function through green infrastructure, and conduct a study to convert an ambitious percentage of City surface parking lots to functional green uses where feasible.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Reclaim public space and prioritize green infrastructure

Identify and reclaim mono-use, underused, and unconventional public spaces... with a focus on green infrastructure.

...A study should be conducted to examine the use of surface-level parking lots on City property to develop a plan to convert an ambitious percentage of these to functional green uses when feasible.
subs.parent_policy_name: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
subs.section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete land-use/green infrastructure actions including a specified study and conversion planning.

subs.policy_statement: Promote community stewardship of neighborhood public lands by establishing Neighborhood Stewardship Councils/Ambassadors, providing paid training/compensation, and creating grant assistance and staff positions to support excluded communities.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Promote community stewardship

Promote community stewardship and management of neighborhood public lands to ensure higher levels of care and maintenance.

How we'll get there:

- Establish Neighborhood Stewardship Councils or Ambassadors...
subs.parent_policy_name: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
subs.section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific governance/program design including paid roles and funding support mechanisms.

subs.policy_statement: Explore carbon farming via food forests and community gardens on public lands using low-carbon practices, including supporting local research in partnership with institutions and organizations serving low-income communities and communities of color.
subs.verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Promote carbon farming

Explore the ability of food forests and community gardens on public lands to use low-carbon and carbon farming practices.
subs.parent_policy_name: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
subs.section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to explore and support carbon farming practices on public lands.

individual.policy_statement: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
individual.verbatim_text: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
individual.extraction_rationale: A quantified, time-bound citywide emissions target (net-zero by 2040).

section_header: on february 15, 2024, the austin city council passed a resolution updating this plan

individual.policy_statement: Austin City Council (Feb. 15, 2024) passed a resolution updating the Austin Climate Equity Plan to add telework/remote work policy content and to update the plan’s per-capita waste disposal goal language to align with the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan.
individual.verbatim_text:
On February 15, 2024, the Austin City Council passed a resolution updating this plan to include telework policies and the per capita waste disposal goal passed in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan. The plan amendments include:

- Page 59 : Transportation and Land Use, Goal 1, Strategy 4 . Under the header 'How we'll get there,' the following language was added:
- o Ensure all City policies for reduced parking, remote work, and telecommuting align with the goals of the Imagine Austin Comprehensive Plan and establish Austin as a leader for workplace and environmental policies to reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips.
- o The City of Austin should develop more remote work policies through a collaborative, all-employee process to develop clear goals and measured outcomes to reduce parking demand at City facilities. In developing City policies, Austin should build on the successful Travis County telework policy.
- Page 63 : Transportation and Land Use, Goal 3 . A new strategy was added with the following language:
- o Strategy 7: Promote remote and flexible work policies
individual.section_header: On February 15, 2024, the Austin City Council passed a resolution updating this plan...
individual.extraction_rationale: This is an explicit legislative action (City Council resolution) that updates the plan by adding specific policy content (telework/remote work policies) and revising waste-goal language; it is a concrete, document-supported commitment rather than background or process description.
